campbell.title: Campbell City Council — July 7, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council is holding public hearings on renewing an interim ordinance tied to a state Senate law and on revoking business licenses and tobacco retailer permits for two local shops, California Cigar and Hayes Smoke Shop. The council will also vote on applying for a grant to fund public works projects and on approving new consultant service agreements.
keyTopics: Renewal of interim ordinance implementing a state Senate bill
Revocation of business license and tobacco retailer permit for California Cigar
Revocation of business license and tobacco retailer permit for Hayes Smoke Shop
Public Works Director authorized to submit a grant application
Approval of new consultant service agreements

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to approve financial reports and move forward with an ordinance that will be formally adopted later in May. The council also discussed whether the city should become a charter city and reviewed potential tax options and revenue estimates.
keyTopics: Preliminary approval of the Engineer's Report for fiscal year 2026
New ordinance scheduled for final adoption on May 20, 2026
Public hearing conducted on a resolution of intention
Charter city status—council exploring benefits and providing direction to staff
Preliminary tax options and revenue estimates presented for consideration

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council is handling a mix of routine and notable business: approving past meeting minutes, reviewing February's financial report, and signing off on contracts including a construction deal with QLM, Inc. and a three-year professional services agreement. The most significant item is a court-ordered hearing on appeals, which the council must address as directed by the court.
keyTopics: Construction contract awarded to QLM, Inc.
Resolution directing preparation of a town engineer's report on planned improvements
New three-year professional services agreement
Second amendment to an existing town agreement
Court-ordered hearing on pending appeals

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to handle routine business, including approving minutes from previous Council and committee meetings. Key action items included updating a retail concession agreement at the airport with PRI-San José, LLC, adjusting airport fees and charges, and approving a City Manager trip to Tampa, Florida.
keyTopics: Approval of previous City Council and committee meeting minutes
Amendment to retail merchandising concession agreement with PRI-San José, LLC
Changes to airport fees and charges rates
City Manager's business travel to Tampa, Florida

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to handle several honorary recognitions, including proclamations for National Immigrant Heritage Month, the Kiwanis Club's 100th anniversary, and City Attorney Jennifer Logue's appointment to the judiciary. On the business side, the Council set next year's budget limits, updated financial policy, adopted a 5-year water management and shortage plan, approved the capital improvement program for 2026-27, and reviewed updates on the Public Safety Building, Shoreline Golf Links parking lot repaving, and the South Bay Salt Pond Restoration project.
keyTopics: Proclamations honoring Immigrant Heritage Month, the Kiwanis Club's 100th anniversary, and City Attorney Jennifer Logue's judicial appointment
Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget: appropriations limit, financial policy updates, and capital improvement program
5-Year Urban Water Management Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan adoption
Public Safety Building project and Shoreline Golf Links parking lot repaving updates
South Bay Salt Pond Restoration project and closed-session discussion on potential litigation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — July 21, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: Sunnyvale's City Council held a special study session and regular meeting covering a proposal to stop using chemical pesticides on city-owned or leased properties, along with routine business like approving bill payments and past meeting minutes. Council also considered a library materials contract update and a $1.39 million contract for repairs to a levee at the city's Water Pollution Control Plant.
keyTopics: Proposal to eliminate chemical pesticide use on city-owned or leased property
Contract award of $1,391,426 to Brannon Corporation for Water Pollution Control Plant oxidation pond levee rehabilitation
Amendment to library services agreement with Brodart Co. for printed materials and shelf-ready books
Approval of city bill payments and June 16, 2026 meeting minutes
Environmental review finding (CEQA Class 1 exemption) for the levee repair project

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 21, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met in a closed session to discuss three ongoing lawsuits against the city with their legal counsel. Because these discussions involve active litigation, no public decisions or votes were made during this portion of the meeting.
keyTopics: Lawsuit: Lixin (Leon) Chen, et al. v. City of Cupertino
Lawsuit: Yes in My Backyard, et al. v. City of Cupertino
Lawsuit: Garden Gate Coalition for Mary Avenue Safety v. City of Cupertino
Closed-door legal strategy session with city attorneys

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 23, 2026
santa-clara.summary: This notice covers how Santa Clara residents can take part in the June 23, 2026 City Council meeting rather than listing specific policy items. The meeting is being held in a hybrid format, so people can attend in person or join remotely via Zoom or phone.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format combining in-person and remote participation
Zoom access details, including meeting link, meeting ID, and dial-in phone number
Instructions for submitting written public comment ahead of the meeting
Use of the eComment tool on the City Council Agenda/Legistar page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council will review a proposed change to how federal housing and community development funds are allocated under the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan for the current fiscal year. Councilmembers will also discuss new project or policy ideas they'd like to add to the city's workplan, and consider changing the rules for how a vacant council seat gets filled, including shortening related timelines.
keyTopics: Proposed amendment to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan and 2025-2026 funding allocations
New workplan item requests submitted by individual councilmembers
Proposed ordinance changes to the council vacancy filling process and timeline
Upcoming council meeting calendar and schedule
Approval of previous city council meeting minutes

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council honored retiring city official Bill Gutoff with a formal proclamation, then held a required public hearing on proposed increases to water, wastewater, and garbage utility rates. The Finance Committee asked the full Council to approve updated financial forecasts and reserve policies for the water and wastewater systems, along with new rate schedules affecting residential and commercial customers.
keyTopics: Proclamation honoring Bill Gutoff's retirement
Proposition 218 public hearing on utility rate increases
Water utility rate changes (schedules W-1 through W-4 and W-7) and reserve fund transfers
Wastewater collection rate changes (schedules S-1, S-2, S-6, S-7) and financial forecast approval
Refuse/garbage collection rate updates (schedules R-1 and R-C)
